description_long: This broom features a long, slender wooden handle with a natural light brown color, smoothly tapering into a dark, conical bristle head. The bristle section is compact and appears to be made of stiff, synthetic or natural fibers, tightly bound at the top with two visible dark bands. The handle is straight and round in cross-section, with no visible ergonomic enhancements or grip textures. The transition between the handle and the bristle head is marked by a slight flare, giving the broom a classic, utilitarian appearance. The bristle head is uniform in color and shape, with no visible variation in bristle length or arrangement. There are no visible brand markings, labels, or signs of wear on any part of the broom.
description: A classic broom with a straight wooden handle and a dark, conical bristle head bound by two bands. The design is simple, with no visible wear or branding.
description_view_0: The broom is seen from the front, showing the full length of the straight handle and the symmetrical, conical bristle head.
description_view_1: This view highlights the slight flare at the base of the handle and the even, rounded shape of the bristle head.
description_view_2: The broom is shown from the back, with the handle and bristle head appearing nearly identical to the front view.
description_view_3: A side view reveals the uniform thickness of the handle and the consistent, compact shape of the bristle head.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, round wooden handle with a smooth finish and no visible grip enhancements or end caps.
attribute_bristle_arrangement: Bristles are densely packed in a conical arrangement, appearing uniform in length and color, with no visible angling or fanning.
attribute_mounting_position: Designed to be stored vertically, likely leaned against a wall or hung by the handle, though no dedicated mounting feature is visible.
